perturbation.original_text: 1. LHT agrees not to disclose, except to its employees and consultants with a need to know, and not to use, except for the purposes set forth herein, any information received from HPAC under this Agreement, whether or not it is oral, written or in any other medium and whether or not it is marked as Confidential or Proprietary, and all notes, copies, analyses, compilations, studies, or interpretations that utilize such information, or are prepared, based on, or derived from such information (collectively, the "Confidential Information"). The Confidential Information shall also include any information provided by HPAC to LHT in connection with the possible business combination being considered by the parties prior to the date of this Agreement. The Confidential Information shall be provided for the purposes of discussions on a possible business combination.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. LHT agrees not to disclose, except to its employees and consultants without a need to know, and not to use, except for purposes approved by LHT Legal Counsel, any information received from HPAC under this Agreement, whether or not it is oral, written or in any other medium and whether or not it is marked as Confidential or Proprietary, and all notes, copies, analyses, compilations, studies, or interpretations that utilize such information, or are prepared, based on, or derived from such information (collectively, the "Confidential Information"). The Confidential Information shall also include any information provided by HPAC to LHT regardless of the business combination being considered by the parties prior to the date of this Agreement. The Confidential Information shall be provided for the purposes of general business development.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction to the original agreement by changing the "need to know" basis to "without a need to know" and changing the permitted use of confidential information to only those purposes approved by LHT Legal Counsel. This contradicts the original intent, which was to allow use for the specific purpose of discussing a possible business combination. The modified text also broadens the definition of Confidential Information to include information regardless of the business combination and allows it to be used for 'general business development'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. LHT agrees not to disclose, except to its employees and consultants without a need to know, and not to use, except for purposes approved by LHT Legal Counsel, any information received from HPAC under this Agreement, whether or not it is oral, written or in any other medium and whether or not it is marked as Confidential or Proprietary, and all notes, copies, analyses, compilations, studies, or interpretations that utilize such information, or are prepared, based on, or derived from such information (collectively, the "Confidential Information"). The Confidential Information shall also include any information provided by HPAC to LHT regardless of the business combination being considered by the parties prior to the date of this Agreement. The Confidential Information shall be provided for the purposes of general business development.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions under which LHT can disclose and use HPAC's confidential information. Specifically, it changes 'with a need to know' to 'without a need to know,' which is contradictory. Additionally, the permitted use is changed from 'the purposes set forth herein' (discussions on a business combination) to 'purposes approved by LHT Legal Counsel' and later, 'general business development,' broadening the scope and contradicting the original intent. The contradicted text section is in the same location as the changed text, meaning it is directly overwritten with new obligations that were not included in the original document, and that contradict the original scope. Thus, there is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. This Agreement shall not be assigned by either party without the express written consent of the other party; however, either party may assign this Agreement to its respective successors in interest. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and may be modified only by a written amendment signed by both parties. LHT agrees to comply with all governmental regulations applicable to the Confidential Information, including federal and state securities laws and US Export Control Laws and Regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. This Agreement shall be assigned by either party without the express written consent of the other party; furthermore, neither party can assign this Agreement to its respective successors in interest. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and may be modified only by an oral agreement between both parties. LHT agrees to comply with all governmental regulations applicable to the Confidential Information, including local labor laws and EU Export Control Laws and Regulations.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces a contradiction by first stating the agreement "shall be assigned" without consent, directly contradicting "shall not be assigned without consent." It further restricts assignment to successors in interest. The original agreement can only be modified by a written amendment, however the modified statement says it can only be modified by an oral agreement between both parties. Also, changing the regulations LHT agrees to comply with introduces further discrepancy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. This Agreement shall be assigned by either party without the express written consent of the other party; furthermore, neither party can assign this Agreement to its respective successors in interest. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and may be modified only by an oral agreement between both parties. LHT agrees to comply with all governmental regulations applicable to the Confidential Information, including local labor laws and EU Export Control Laws and Regulations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several contradictions. First, it states that the agreement "shall be assigned" without consent, which directly contradicts the original statement that the agreement "shall not be assigned without consent." Second, it prevents assignment to successors in interest, whereas the original text allowed it. Third, it specifies that the agreement can only be modified by an "oral agreement," contradicting the original requirement of a "written amendment." All of these changes introduce direct conflicts with the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: 6. LHT acknowledges that HPAC will be irreparably harmed if LHT's obligations are not specifically enforced, and that no adequate legal remedy exists for breach thereof. LHT agrees that HPAC shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief, in addition to any other remedies available. Reasonable attorney's fees and costs shall be awarded to HPAC in the event of litigation in which HPAC prevails involving the enforcement or interpretation of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. LHT acknowledges that HPAC will not be irreparably harmed if LHT's obligations are not specifically enforced, and that an adequate legal remedy exists for breach thereof. LHT agrees that HPAC shall not be entitled to obtain injunctive relief, in addition to any other remedies available. Attorney's fees and costs shall be awarded to LHT in the event of litigation in which HPAC prevails involving the enforcement or interpretation of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts the original text by asserting that HPAC will *not* be irreparably harmed and that an adequate legal remedy *does* exist. It also removes HPAC's entitlement to injunctive relief and states the opposite about attorney fees, which are now awarded to LHT even when HPAC wins the litigation, contradicting the original intention and fairness of legal recourse.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. LHT acknowledges that HPAC will not be irreparably harmed if LHT's obligations are not specifically enforced, and that an adequate legal remedy exists for breach thereof. LHT agrees that HPAC shall not be entitled to obtain injunctive relief, in addition to any other remedies available. Attorney's fees and costs shall be awarded to LHT in the event of litigation in which HPAC prevails involving the enforcement or interpretation of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text. The original text stated HPAC would be irreparably harmed if LHT's obligations were not enforced, and HPAC would be entitled to injunctive relief. The changed text states the opposite: HPAC would *not* be irreparably harmed, and LHT would be awarded attorney's fees even when HPAC wins, a direct contradiction. This creates an inconsistency, as per the definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2. LHT agrees to prevent the unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information received hereunder using the same degree of care employed to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than a reasonable standard of care.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. LHT agrees to permit the unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information received hereunder using the same degree of care employed to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than a reasonable standard of care.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "prevent the unauthorized disclosure or use" to "permit the unauthorized disclosure or use" creates a direct contradiction. The original intent was to protect the confidential information, while the changed text implies acceptance or allowance of unauthorized disclosure. This creates legal ambiguity concerning LHT's responsibilities regarding the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. LHT agrees to permit the unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information received hereunder using the same degree of care employed to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than a reasonable standard of care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'prevent the unauthorized disclosure or use' to 'permit the unauthorized disclosure or use'. This creates a direct contradiction because the original text aims to protect confidential information, while the changed text implies allowing unauthorized disclosure. Since the changed text and contradicted text are the same, the contradiction is clear and significant, creating legal ambiguity as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.
